description: A patient, hungry liquid that whispers to all it touches, give way, give way.
The rarefied breath of the heavens, the fifth element said to fill the space between stars.
Ancient sap turned to golden stone, forever cradling whatever once wandered into it.
Stone that grew into geometry, catching the light and bending it into quiet rainbows.
The hardest, purest jewel the deep can yield, a sliver of starlight pressed into stone.
The deathless draught, bottled life itself, sought by every alchemist who ever feared the grave.
The first and fastest of all things, pure radiance given shape against the dark.
The sky's sudden fury, a jagged heartbeat of raw power written across the storm.
A still pool of frozen light that steals your face and offers it politely back.
A slow secret grown in the dark, the sea's patient answer to a single grain of grief.
The legendary prize of all alchemy, perfection itself, said to turn lead to gold and death to life.
The fourth and fiercest state of being, raw matter unbound and blazing like a caged star.
The shape that light leaves behind, neither thing nor nothing, clinging to all that is.
The hungry nothing between worlds, a doorway opening onto everything that is not.
The first breath of the world, restless and unseen, forever slipping between your fingers.
Patient and ancient, the bones of creation that hold everything else in place.
A hungry spark that dances, devours, and remembers every secret it burns.
The wanderer of the elements, shaping all it touches while keeping no shape of its own.
The grey memory of fire, all that remains when the dancing flame finally tires.
The quiet scaffold of the once-living, whispering of bodies long returned to dust.
Bitter seawater wrung to its salty soul, the ocean distilled into a single sharp tear.
Buried sunlight, patient and black, waiting an age for the chance to burn again.
The world ground small, a thousand forgotten things adrift on a single beam of light.
Winter's delicate handwriting, lacing the world in fragile, glittering lace.
The earth's molten heartbeat, slow and furious, remaking the land wherever it crawls.
The breath of morning made visible, a soft grey veil that hides as much as it reveals.
Earth and water in their oldest argument, sticky, humble, and the cradle of small life.
Raw promise locked in stone, the sleeping metal that dreams of fire and form.
A scrap of captured spring, soft and bright, holding the faintest perfume of life.
The taste of tears and tides, a humble crystal that preserves what time would steal.
The desert's countless hourglass grains, each one a mountain worn down by patience.
Fire's restless ghost, climbing toward the sky to carry secrets no one can read.
Water freed from its weight, a hissing spirit rushing upward to rejoin the clouds.
Time made solid, steadfast and silent, keeping the long patience of the mountains.
The slow green ambition of the forest, sunlight and seasons woven into living grain.
Humble mud tempered by fire into something proud, ringing clear when it is struck.
The distilled spark of living things, a bottled whisper of the breath of life.
Salt that swallowed a flame and never let go, smouldering with a buried, bitter heat.
Life turned to patient stone, a creature's last secret kept for a thousand ages.
Sand seduced by fire into clarity, fragile, gleaming, and honest to a fault.
The metal that never tarnishes, hoarded by kings and dragons and dreamed of by fools.
Water caught mid-thought and frozen there, sharp, clear, and quietly merciless.
The stubborn backbone of the forge, plain but unyielding, the maker of a hundred tools.
Lava kissed by sudden water, frozen into black glass with an edge keener than regret.
A liquid metal that refuses to be held, trembling, mirror-bright, and ever restless.
Iron's slow surrender to air and rain, a flaking orange ruin that was once so proud.
Iron schooled by fire and coal until it learned to be both sharp and unbreakable.
